title: aquilegia vulgaris var. corniculata
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. corniculata, commonly known as the red columbine, is a flowering plant species within the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of red columbine include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a valuable remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
